description: Installation command reads `uv uv pip install scikit-learn` — the doubled `uv` prefix is an invalid shell invocation that will fail immediately for users copying this command
suggested_fix: Replace `uv uv pip install scikit-learn` with `uv pip install scikit-learn`

description: Installation command reads `uv uv pip install pyopenms` — the doubled `uv` prefix is an invalid shell invocation that will fail immediately for users copying this command
suggested_fix: Replace `uv uv pip install pyopenms` with `uv pip install pyopenms`

description: Installation command reads `uv uv pip install fluidsim` (appears 3 times in installation section) — the doubled `uv` prefix is an invalid shell invocation
suggested_fix: Replace all 3 instances of `uv uv pip install fluidsim` with `uv pip install fluidsim`

description: Installation command reads `uv uv pip install gtars` — the doubled `uv` prefix is an invalid shell invocation
suggested_fix: Replace `uv uv pip install gtars` with `uv pip install gtars`

description: Installation commands read `uv uv pip install geniml`, `uv uv pip install 'geniml[ml]'`, and `uv uv pip install git+https://...` — doubled `uv` prefix is invalid in all three forms
suggested_fix: Remove the leading `uv ` from all three install commands: `uv pip install geniml`, `uv pip install 'geniml[ml]'`, `uv pip install git+https://github.com/databio/geniml.git`

description: Installation command reads `python3 -m uv pip install latch` — calling uv as a Python module is not the standard invocation and may fail depending on how uv is installed
suggested_fix: Replace `python3 -m uv pip install latch` with `uv pip install latch`

description: frontmatter contains `license: SD-3-Clause license` — the leading `B` is missing from `BSD-3-Clause`. This is an apparent typo that results in an unrecognized SPDX identifier.
suggested_fix: Change `license: SD-3-Clause license` to `license: BSD-3-Clause`

description: SKILL.md contains mandatory workflow instructions referencing 'Nano Banana 2' and 'Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview' — K-Dense Inc.'s proprietary paid AI image generation product. Skills invoking this workflow fail silently without an undisclosed paid API key (OPENROUTER_API_KEY). The description does not mention this requirement.
suggested_fix: Either document the API key requirement prominently in the frontmatter description, or replace proprietary branding with the generic tool abstraction already present in the scripts themselves

description: SKILL.md contains mandatory instructions to call 'Nano Banana Pro' for schematic generation, implying this is a standard capability when it requires a paid API key not disclosed in the skill description
suggested_fix: Document API key requirement in description or replace proprietary branding with tool-agnostic wording

description: Script makes POST requests to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 transmitting user diagram prompt text and receiving AI-generated images. API key sourced from OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var (correct practice). Risk: user natural-language prompts leave the local machine to a commercial third-party service. Skill descriptions referencing this script do not disclose data transmission.
suggested_fix: Add disclosure to affected SKILL.md descriptions: note that diagram generation sends prompt text to openrouter.ai. Approximately 14 copies of this script exist across skills.

description: Script sends user research query text to https://api.parallel.ai (PARALLEL_API_KEY) and optionally to OpenRouter Perplexity backend (OPENROUTER_API_KEY). Two separate commercial services receive user query content. The research-lookup SKILL.md description does not disclose that queries are transmitted externally.
suggested_fix: Add data-transmission disclosure to research-lookup/SKILL.md description, noting that queries are sent to api.parallel.ai and openrouter.ai
